raw_consumer_statement:
CFPB Consumer Rebuttal Statement I strongly disagree with Citis response and the decision to close this case. Citi has mischaracterized the issue.

I am not disputing the {$1700.00} balance for the upgraded TV purchased on XX/XX/XXXX. I am disputing that Citi has failed to remove the original {$1600.00} charge from XX/XX/XXXX, despite the fact that this TV was returned and exchanged, as documented in my receipt dated XX/XX/XXXX. 

Citis letter claims a refund of {$840.00} was applied against the new purchase, leaving {$1700.00} due. That does not match the actual charges on my statements. The disputed {$1600.00} remains unreversed, resulting in me being billed for two televisions when only one was kept.

I have attached the documentation again : Best Buy receipt showing the return/exchange Billing statement showing the {$1600.00} charge still applied This is a duplicate billing error under the Fair Credit Billing Act. I request CFPBs assistance in requiring Citi to properly reverse the {$1600.00} charge, correct my account balance, and provide written confirmation of the adjustment.

Bottom line : I returned one TV and should not have to pay for two. The {$1600.00} charge must be removed.
